title: vinta-ai-workflows project config
description: Single source of truth for vinta-ai-workflows configuration in a target project. Lives at `.vinta-ai-workflows.yaml` at the repo root. Authored by `vinta-bootstrap-ai-tools`; updated in place by `vinta-sync-ai-tools`. Read by every builder skill, foundation skill template, and meta-skill. See schemas/README.md for versioning rules.

schema_version.description: Schema major version. Bumped only on breaking changes.

vinta_ai_workflows_version.description: Last `vinta-ai-workflows` package version this project synced from. Compared against the cloned package version by `vinta-sync-ai-tools` to enumerate releases since.

last_synced_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last successful bootstrap or sync run.

stack_summary.description: Human-readable one-liner of the stack (lang + framework + key deps). Pulled from `vinta-analyze-codebase` inventory.

ai_plans_dir.description: Directory holding `YYYY-MM-DD-FEATURE_NAME_{SPEC,PLAN}.md` files. Default `ai-plans`. Monorepos often nest it under a service dir (e.g. `apps/<service>/ai-plans`).

pr_template_paths.description: Paths (relative to repo root) of the project's existing PR / MR templates. Detected at bootstrap by scanning for `.github/pull_request_template.md`, `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`, `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/*.md`, `pull_request_template.md`, `docs/pull_request_template.md` (GitHub case-insensitive) and `.gitlab/merge_request_templates/*.md` (GitLab). When non-empty, `implement-plan` / `amend-plan` use the template as the scaffold for the prs-context file's `# Description` section. Empty array → free-form description. Multiple entries (template dir) → orchestrator asks the user which to use, or picks `default.md` if present.

commands.description: Commands consumed by `implement-plan` / `amend-plan` / project foundation skills at runtime.

build.description: Type-check / build gate. Often the same as the test command on Python projects.

test_unit_scoped.description: Per-package or per-app scoped suite (e.g. `pnpm test:patient`).

test_unit_new_pattern.description: Pattern for running a single new test file in isolation. Use placeholders like `<pkg>` / `<new-test-path>`.

policies.description: Step-0 interview answers driving template rendering + agent prompts.

pr_creation.description: Whether agents open PRs or only push branches. Drives the `implement-plan` skill's `Open PR via context file` step matrix.

ai_coauthor.description: Whether commits may carry AI co-author trailers (`Co-Authored-By: Claude ...`).

stage_pattern.description: Default `git add` pattern shown to subagents (no `git add -A`).

anti_git_add_all_reason.description: One-line reason `git add -A` is forbidden in this repo (e.g. secrets, build artifacts in tree).

dependency_licenses.description: Third-party dependency license policy. Captured at bootstrap; rendered into `ai-tools/AGENTS.md` (`## Dependency licenses` section) and the `implement-plan` / `amend-plan` skill bodies so the implementer / reviewer agents check a new dep's SPDX license against `forbidden_spdx` before running any install command (`npm add`, `pnpm add`, `pip install`, `poetry add`, `uv add`, `cargo add`, `go get`, etc.). When `enforcement: block`, the agent must refuse the install and surface the violation to the user; `warn` records the violation in the phase report but proceeds; `off` skips the check entirely.

enforcement.description: How the implementer / reviewer agents react when a new dep's license is in `forbidden_spdx`. `block` (default) — refuse install, ask user to confirm override before proceeding. `warn` — proceed but flag in the phase report. `off` — skip the check.

forbidden_spdx.description: SPDX license identifiers (https://spdx.org/licenses/) the project refuses to take on as a third-party dep. Bootstrap proposes a default list (GPL-2.0-only, GPL-3.0-only, AGPL-3.0-only, SSPL-1.0) targeting viral copyleft licenses; the user confirms / edits before write. Empty array disables the check even when `enforcement` is `block` or `warn` (use `enforcement: off` instead for clarity).

allowed_overrides.description: Explicit per-package exceptions to `forbidden_spdx`. Each entry whitelists one package + license pair the team has accepted (legal review, dual-license clause, in-house carve-out). The implementer agent skips the block when the candidate package + license matches an entry; the reviewer agent treats the entry as load-bearing context (don't silently drop).

package.description: Package identifier as it appears in the manifest (npm name, PyPI distribution, Cargo crate, Go module path).

license.description: SPDX identifier this override applies to. Must be present in `forbidden_spdx` for the override to mean anything.

reason.description: One-line justification (e.g. 'legal review 2025-Q4', 'GPL exception clause covers our use', 'wraps a CLI invoked at build time only — not linked').

notes.description: Free-form prose surfaced verbatim in the AGENTS.md `## Dependency licenses` section. Use for nuance the structured fields can't capture (e.g. 'LGPL is fine for dynamic linking but not for static linking — check the consuming module before adding', 'all viral-license deps go through @counsel before merge').

vendors.description: Vendor coverage selected at bootstrap. Consumed by `setup-ai-tools.mjs` `--only` flag.

foundation_skills.description: Per-foundation-skill opt-in. Use `enabled` / `disabled`. `disabled` is a sticky opt-out — `vinta-sync-ai-tools` does not re-propose disabled skills.

add-one-off-script.description: Author safe operational one-off scripts (backfills, cleanups, ad-hoc data fixes) following a strict contract: dry-run by default, idempotent on re-run, batched DB operations, streamed reads, segmented CSV backups before destructive writes (max 1M cells/file, never nested across tables), interruption-safe signal handlers, and console + filesystem + S3 logging. Bundles a `BaseOneOffScript` class template under [resources/foundation-skills/add-one-off-script/resources/](resources/foundation-skills/add-one-off-script/resources/). When `enabled`, project-level configuration lives under `skills.add-one-off-script` (scripts dir, S3 bucket/prefix, default batch size).

systematic-debugging.description: Root-cause-first debugging skill rendered from `vinta-derive-skills/resources/systematic-debugging-template.md`. When `enabled`, the per-project MCP tool selection lives in `skills.systematic-debugging.observability_mcp_tools`.

foundation_agents.description: Per-foundation-agent opt-in. Foundation trio (`implementer` / `reviewer` / `fixer`) are usually `enabled`.

stack_specialist_agents.description: Stack-specific sub-agents shipped (e.g. `migration-author` for Django, `deploy-author` for Medplum).

run_options.description: Per-skill run-option defaults. Skills override via per-invocation prompts; values here are the defaults shown.

blast_radius_signal_threshold.description: How many tripping signals before suggesting restart-from-scratch. 2 = `high-blast-radius`, 3 = `restart-recommended`.

allow_local_only_debug.description: Whether the user is permitted to opt out of the MCP preflight + categories block on a per-run basis (e.g. `/systematic-debugging local-only — flaky test`). Defaults to `true`. Set to `false` for projects where production-only bugs must always go through the observability sweep — the skill will then refuse the `local-only` keyword and require the user to wire up an MCP server (or remove `systematic-debugging` from the project) instead.

skills.description: Per-skill custom config. Open schema — each skill defines its own keys.

systematic-debugging.description: Configuration for the `systematic-debugging` foundation skill. Required when `foundation_skills.systematic-debugging` is `enabled`; ignored otherwise.

observability_mcp_servers.description: Free-form list of MCP server identifiers the agent should introspect during Phase 0 (evidence sweep) before any hypothesis. Identifiers use whatever shorthand the team recognises (e.g. `sentry`, `datadog`, `our-internal-traces`, `grafana-prod`). No enum is enforced — the systematic-debugging agent discovers specific tool names + capabilities at runtime by listing the tools each server exposes and matching them against the evidence categories baked into the rendered SKILL.md (see `vinta-derive-skills/resources/systematic-debugging-mcp-tools.md`). Empty array is allowed but degrades the skill — the rendered Phase 0 collapses to 'local logs only'. Order is preserved in the rendered list; not significant otherwise.

add-one-off-script.description: Configuration for the `add-one-off-script` foundation skill. Optional when `foundation_skills.add-one-off-script` is `enabled` — sensible defaults apply when omitted.

scripts_dir.description: Where one-off scripts live in the repo. Default `scripts/one_off`. Override for monorepos that nest scripts under a service / package dir.

language.description: Primary language for scripts; selects which `BaseOneOffScript` template to stage. Default inferred from project stack at bootstrap.

log_dir.description: Where logs, PID files, processed-items logs, and CSV backups land. Default `.vinta-ai-workflows/one-off-runs` (gitignored).

default_batch_size.description: Default batch size injected into the `BaseOneOffScript` config when scripts don't override. Smaller for wide rows / contended tables.

csv_max_cells.description: Per-CSV-chunk cell cap (rows × columns) before the writer rolls over to the next file. The contract requires segmented CSVs so a single backup chunk stays loadable without OOM.

s3_bucket.description: S3 bucket for log + CSV-backup uploads. Falls back to the `ONE_OFF_S3_BUCKET` env var at runtime when unset. Empty string disables S3 upload (filesystem copy remains authoritative).

s3_prefix.description: S3 key prefix for uploads. Falls back to `ONE_OFF_S3_PREFIX` env, default `one-off-runs/`. Trailing slash recommended.
